title: Cercouratea cassinifolia
taxonomy: Cercouratea cassinifolia is classified in the family Celastraceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to Madagascar and belongs to the genus Cercouratea. The classification of this plant places it within the order Celastrales. The family Celastraceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the bittersweet or staff vine family. Cercouratea cassinifolia's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in its family.

Its medicinal uses include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and bark are often employed in folk remedies to alleviate symptoms. Research on Cercouratea cassinifolia suggests potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
